mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs and a higher discount rate for future corporate earnings compress equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which increases the risk of wage-driven inflation and subsequent monetary policy tightening, negatively impacting equity market forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong retail sales growth and high inflation signals an overheating economy. This forces the central bank to maintain or increase policy restrictiveness, leading to negative equity returns as financial conditions tighten.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year change in RSAFS > 3% AND year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL > 4%
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in a majority of months where both retail sales growth and inflation are above their trigger thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, a positive surprise in industrial production signals a beneficial supply-side expansion. This alleviates inflation concerns and boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is in a restrictive regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3%, positive monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding a high threshold signals an exceptionally hot labor market, raising fears of wage inflation and a hawkish central bank response, thus negatively impacting equity prices.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS print above 300k is not statistically significantly lower than the average return following prints below 300k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for a slowdown in broader economic activity and consumer demand, negatively affecting future corporate earnings and equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: A 3-month moving average of HOUST changes shows no significant negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is already high, a further tightening of the labor market (falling unemployment) removes any possibility of a dovish pivot from the central bank, forcing it to maintain a restrictive policy stance that is negative for equities.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 4%, months with falling UNRATE are not followed by statistically significant negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter deceleration in real GDP growth signals a loss of economic momentum, leading to downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and causing negative equity market returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The information coefficient between the quarterly change in GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently negative over rolling 5-year windows.
